opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Hard-coded OS integration creates a false sense of privacy - the moment you need agent persistence across sessions, you're back in the cloud, which defeats the local execution selling point. The real engineering challenge isn't the agent intelligence, but designing a permission sandbox that's both secure enough for system-level access and permissive enough for useful automation.**
actions.reasoning: I have direct hands-on experience building multi-provider AI agents, tool orchestration systems, and cognitive architectures, including the specific tradeoffs between local execution vs. cloud integration that this post touches on.**
